summary: PR #2640 reworked source shapes in the VS Records mnDiagram translation unit, primarily in sorting, popup, cursor, and table-rendering code. The PR body reports mndiagram .text fuzzy match improvement from 92.70628% to 96.47131%, matched functions from 30 to 33, and .sdata2 improvement from 84.09090% to 88.63636%. The bot report confirms 3 new function matches and 10 additional improvements in main/melee/mn/mndiagram.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source change for mnDiagram sorting, input, popup, cursor, animation-table field names, helper inlines, PAD_STACK tuning, and control-flow reshaping.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c added 435 deleted 431 across 43 hunks
diff: adds GET_DIAGRAM macro and renames mnDiagram_AnimTable x58/x64 to arrow_anim/cursor_anim
diff: reworks mnDiagram_8023FA6C, mnDiagram_8023FC28, mnDiagram_InputProc, mnDiagram_80241310, mnDiagram_802417D0

key_files.role: Small header/static-surface update; loaded metadata shows 2 added lines, but the provided diff excerpt does not include exact content.
evidence_refs: changed_files: src/melee/mn/mndiagram2.static.h added 2 deleted 0 across 1 hunk

what_changed.text: Reworked mnDiagram sorting helpers and source shape for fighter/name ranking. The diff adds static inline helpers for summing fighter KOs, summing name KOs, and checking whether all play times are zero, then uses them in mnDiagram_8023FA6C, mnDiagram_8023FC28, and mnDiagram_GetLeastPlayedFighter.
Introduced GET_DIAGRAM(gobj) as a local macro around HSD_GObjGetUserData and used it in mnDiagram_InputProc and mnDiagram_80241310 instead of direct user_data access from mnDiagram_804D6C10.

Separated popup and main-overlay user-data layouts by changing mnDiagram_PopupData text[6] to text[5], adding STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(mnDiagram_PopupData) == 0x4C), and adding mnDiagram_MainOverlay with text[6].
Renamed animation table fields x58 and x64 to arrow_anim and cursor_anim and updated animation calls to use those names.
Reworked mnDiagram_802417D0 arrow visibility logic from label/goto-heavy loops toward structured while/do-while control flow while retaining limited gotos for shared exits.
evidence_refs: diff: mnDiagram_802417D0 replaces loop labels with while/count logic and right_done/fc_right_done/dn_name_done exits

what_changed.text: Kept or adjusted matching tactics including PAD_STACK size changes and a scoped pragma block around mnDiagram_802417D0.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK(16) -> PAD_STACK(12) in sorting functions
diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_reuse_strings off before mnDiagram_802417D0
pr_body: verification lists python configure.py && ninja, ninja build/GALE01/main.dol, melee-agent hook validate --verbose

what_changed.text: The PR body explicitly says mndiagram constants were kept materialized locally so .sdata2 would not regress.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Keep mndiagram constants materialized locally so `.sdata2` does not regress

smart_moves.text: The PR paired source-shape refactors with narrow reported match deltas for each affected function, making the improvement searchable and reviewable.
evidence_refs: pr_body: Function deltas list 12 mnDiagram functions
bot_report: confirms 3 new matches and 10 improvements

smart_moves.text: Instead of leaving generic x58/x64 names, the PR used observed call roles to name animation table members arrow_anim and cursor_anim.
evidence_refs: diff: arrow_anim used for scroll arrow JObjs, cursor_anim used in popup cursor animation

smart_moves.text: The PR separated similar-looking user-data structs rather than forcing one struct layout to cover both popup and main overlay users.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For menu table/ranking code, extracting repeated sum/check loops into small static inline helpers can be a viable source hypothesis when the helper names correspond to local roles and the PR carries function-level match evidence.
When one blob of user_data has multiple actual layouts, split the local struct types and add size checks instead of stretching one struct across all users.
Generated-looking label ladders in cursor/scroll checks can often be converted to structured loops, but this PR still kept narrow goto exits where needed for the matching shape.
evidence_refs: diff: mnDiagram_802417D0 replaces loop labels with while/do-while logic and retains right_done/fc_right_done/dn_name_done labels

naming_conventions.name: GET_DIAGRAM(gobj)
naming_conventions.rationale: Wraps HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj) cast to Diagram* for repeated mnDiagram user-data access.
evidence_refs: diff: GET_DIAGRAM used in mnDiagram_InputProc and mnDiagram_80241310

naming_conventions.name: arrow_anim
naming_conventions.rationale: Renames mnDiagram_AnimTable x58 where the field is passed to arrow animation/visibility calls.
evidence_refs: diff: mnDiagram_802417D0 passes &tbl->arrow_anim to mn_8022ED6C for arrow JObjs

naming_conventions.name: cursor_anim
naming_conventions.rationale: Renames mnDiagram_AnimTable x64 where the field drives popup cursor animation and end-frame comparison.

naming_conventions.name: mnDiagram_PopupData / mnDiagram_MainOverlay
naming_conventions.rationale: Uses separate names for two local user-data layouts with similar jobj arrays but different text array counts.

naming_conventions.name: mnDiagram_SumFighterKOs, mnDiagram_SumNameKOs, mnDiagram_AllPlayTimesZero
naming_conventions.rationale: Helper names describe local aggregate or predicate roles recovered from loops.

notes: Evidence is strong for PR identity, changed files, reported function/section deltas, automated bot report, and visible mndiagram.c edits.
The raw diff is truncated in loaded_files and the excerpt does not show the mndiagram2.static.h hunk, so no exact lesson is extracted from that header change.
No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded metadata.
Verification commands are author-reported in the PR body; the bot report independently confirms match deltas but does not validate every stated command.
Some tactics, especially PAD_STACK and pragma usage, are visible but lack per-tactic rationale in the PR slice; they are recorded as tactics with caution rather than promoted standards.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split local user-data structs when one inferred layout is covering two actual menu objects
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2640 provides evidence that mnDiagram popup data and main overlay data needed separate local struct types: mnDiagram_PopupData uses text[5] with a size assert, while mnDiagram_MainOverlay uses text[6]. This improved reviewability and was part of a PR with function-level match gains.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Small static inline aggregate helpers can be matching-positive for repeated menu ranking loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2640 added mnDiagram_SumFighterKOs, mnDiagram_SumNameKOs, and mnDiagram_AllPlayTimesZero and used them in ranking/sorting code. The PR body and bot report tie this area to new matches and improvements, especially mnDiagram_8023FA6C and mnDiagram_GetLeastPlayedFighter reaching 100%.
evidence_refs: bot_report: new matches in mnDiagram_8023FA6C and mnDiagram_GetLeastPlayedFighter

source_update_candidates.title: Document caution for keeping local constants when .sdata2 would regress
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2640 states that mndiagram constants were kept materialized locally so .sdata2 would not regress, and the bot report shows .sdata2 improvement. Curator should decide whether this is already covered by literals-and-data-ownership/text-before-data-matching or whether a narrow note about local materialization under section evidence is useful.

source_update_candidates.reason: This may be a useful refinement to existing literal/data standards, but the slice does not show exact constants or ownership details, so it should not be promoted automatically.

rejection_notes.text: Do not generalize PR #2640 into a rule that direct __assert expansion is preferred over HSD_ASSERTREPORT. The diff shows one expanded OSReport/__assert shape for mnDiagram_80241310, but loaded standards generally prefer assert/report macros when they match local idioms.
evidence_refs: diff: HSD_ASSERTREPORT replaced by OSReport plus __assert

rejection_notes.text: Do not promote PAD_STACK(12), PAD_STACK(72), or pragma dont_reuse_strings as general style from this PR. They are visible matching tactics with reported overall verification, but no per-tactic rationale is included in the slice.
Do not extract exact facts about src/melee/mn/mndiagram2.static.h beyond changed-file metadata; its diff content is not visible in the loaded excerpt.
